author: bridge-bot
content: <Jommi> if only layerzero used lightclients huh

author: bridge-bot
content: <etan_status> or any larger setup than 1/1, tbh. lightclient as a 2/2 is always nice, though, because you get an extra entity controlling the second factor

author: bridge-bot
content:
<jorkleo> re: **@Jommi**
> if only layerzero used lightclients huh
What you need is a client that does local verification —> colibri stateless 
https://github.com/corpus-core/colibri-stateless

author: bridge-bot
content: <etan_status> the tricky part is getting colibri / nimbus verified proxy / helios into the smart contract that accepts the data

author: bridge-bot
content: <etan_status> ethereum data structures today are simply not very efficient to prove, and efforts to make them more efficient have been lower priority compared to scaling efforts

author: bridge-bot
content:
<stkux> re: **@etan_status**
> the tricky part is getting colibri / nimbus verified proxy / helios into the smart contract that accepts the data
What do you mean with that? Of course, there could be optimizations to make it more efficient. But proofing state, logs, eth_call, etc. together with efficient consensus proof is possible, as it is implemented in colibri.stateless (see https://corpuscore.tech)

author: bridge-bot
content: <etan_status> higher gas cost, compared to status quo

author: bridge-bot
content: <etan_status> "there could be optimizations to make it more efficient" -> due to this, essentially
